source_jobs.title: Technical Support Associate
source_jobs.description:
About Wealth.com http://Wealth.com

Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is the industry’s leading AI Wealth Management platform, empowering financial advisors to offer modern, comprehensive estate planning solutions to their clients. By combining cutting-edge technology, AI, and unmatched human expertise, we deliver a seamless experience that sets us apart. Beyond having one of the most coveted domain names in FinTech, wealth.com http://wealth.com is a dynamic, revenue-generating startup experiencing rapid yet sustainable growth.

A key reason behind our position as the #1 estate planning platform is our team. Our people thrive in a collaborative, supportive culture that fosters innovation and makes wealth.com http://wealth.com a place where everyone truly enjoys working. Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is certified as a Great Place to Work for 2024 and named a 2024 finalist for Fast Company’s Best Workplaces for Innovators!



About the Role

We’re looking for a technically fluent Support Associate to join our growing support team — specifically to help cover East Coast hours where we have a critical gap in support volume. This is not a traditional L1 ticket-handling role. Our AI support bot handles the bulk of routine inquiries, which means your time will be spent on the complex, technical, and nuanced issues that require real troubleshooting depth.

You’ll work closely with Product and Engineering, serve as a technical escalation point for the support team, and help drive improvements to our platform through quality feedback loops. If you’re someone who gets energized by digging into a tricky bug, reading logs, and coordinating with engineers to ship a fix — this role was built for you.



What You’ll Do

 - Serve as the technical escalation point for complex client issues — diagnosing, reproducing, and resolving problems that go beyond standard documentation.

 - Read and interpret application logs, error traces to identify root causes and communicate findings clearly to Engineering.

 - Triage and manage integration-related issues across third-party platforms and coordinating with Product and Engineering through to resolution.

 - Partner closely with Engineering and Product teams on bug escalations, feature requests, and platform improvements — acting as the voice of the client internally.

 - Respond to client support requests via phone, email, and live chat (EST hours) with a high degree of technical accuracy and professionalism.

 - Investigate and document issues that fall outside existing runbooks, contributing to a growing internal knowledge base and escalation playbooks.

 - Actively contribute to AI support system improvements — analyzing edge cases, flagging gaps in bot coverage, and helping refine our chatbot’s effectiveness.

 - Utilize tools including Intercom, Salesforce, and internal dashboards to manage ticket workflows and maintain visibility across open issues.



What We’re Looking For

 - 3+ years of experience in technical support, application support, or a customer-facing engineering-adjacent role.

 - Demonstrated ability to read and interpret logs, and error messages — you don’t need to be handed a solution, you can find one.

 - Experience working with integrations, webhooks, or third-party platform troubleshooting strongly preferred.

 - Track record of collaborating directly with Engineering and Product teams on escalations, bug reports, and feature feedback.

 - Background in financial services, wealthtech, legaltech, or SaaS platforms is a strong plus — estate planning experience is a bonus.

 - Comfortable operating in a fast-paced startup environment with evolving priorities and limited process documentation.

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills — you can translate a technical root cause into plain language for a non-technical client.

 - East Coast-based or fully available to work EST hours.

 - Familiarity with tools such as Intercom, Salesforce, Datadog, or similar support/observabili

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer - AI/ML
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is the industry’s leading estate planning platform, empowering more than 1,000 wealth management firms to modernize how they talk about estate planning with their clients. As the only tech-led, end-to-end platform built specifically for financial institutions, Wealth.com http://Wealth.com enables firms to drive scale, efficiency, and measurable client impact. Trusted by some of the largest names in finance, Wealth.com http://Wealth.com combines proprietary AI, robust security, and deep technological and legal expertise to serve the full range of client needs, from foundational plans to the most sophisticated estate strategies. The company has been widely recognized for innovation and leadership, winning Top Estate Planning Technology and Top Estate Planning Implementation at the 2025 WealthManagement.com http://WealthManagement.com Industry Awards, being named the 2024 Best Technology Provider in the Trust category, and earning #1 in estate planning market share in the 2025 Kitces AdvisorTech Study.

Our team is fundamental to our standing as the leading estate planning platform. We cultivate a collaborative and supportive environment, fostering innovation and making Wealth.com http://Wealth.com a truly enjoyable workplace. Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is proud to be certified as a Great Place to Work for 2025.



The Role

We are seeking a Software Engineer, AI/ML (Full-Stack) to build the next generation of AI-powered applications. This is a builder role. You will design and ship production-grade, agentic systems that deliver real user value — not prototypes or demos.



You will operate at the intersection of AI, product, and full-stack engineering, taking ambiguous ideas and turning them into scalable, user-facing experiences. From document understanding to multi-step workflows, your work will directly shape how users interact with AI.



What You Will Do:


OWN PROBLEMS END-TO-END

 - Take loosely defined ideas and turn them into shipped, production features

 - Make architectural decisions across AI systems, backend, and frontend


BUILD REAL AI PRODUCTS (NOT DEMOS)

 - Design, build, and deploy production-grade systems leveraging LLMs

 - Own systems that handle real user traffic, constraints, and failure modes


DEVELOP AGENTIC SYSTEMS

 - Build multi-step workflows with reasoning, tool use, and structured outputs

 - Design systems that balance autonomy, control, and reliability


DESIGN AI-NATIVE USER EXPERIENCES

 - Build AI-native interfaces (agentic UIs) where users collaborate with AI

 - Implement streaming, feedback loops, and iterative workflows


WORK WITH TOOLS / SKILLS (AI CAPABILITIES)

 - Integrate and compose tools (“skills”) such as APIs, retrieval systems, and external actions

 - Design product features that leverage these capabilities effectively within agent workflows

 - Collaborate with AI Infrastructure to improve tool interfaces and behavior


DRIVE QUALITY AND PERFORMANCE

 - Build evaluation and feedback loops

 - Implement guardrails and monitoring

 - Optimize for latency, cost, and accuracy


WORK ACROSS THE STACK

 - Backend services, APIs, data pipelines, and frontend experiences

 - Ship quickly and iterate based on real usage



Qualifications:

 - Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience

 - Strong software engineering fundamentals (system design, data structures, clean code)

 - Proven track record of building and shipping production systems

 - Proficiency in Python, TypeScript, C#, and comfort working across a polyglot stack, picking up new languages and frameworks as needed

 - Experience building AI-powered applications in production

 - Hands-on experience with agentic or multi-step AI systems

 - Experience working with APIs or tool integrations within AI systems

 - Ability to operate in ambiguous, fast-moving environments



Preferred Qualifications (Bonus Points):

 - Experience building end-to-end agen

source_jobs.title: Applied Scientist- AI/ML Intern
source_jobs.description:
Title: Applied Scientist AI/ML Intern
Location: Remote/United States



About Us

Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is the industry’s leading estate planning platform, empowering more than 1,000 wealth management firms to modernize how they talk about estate planning with their clients. As the only tech-led, end-to-end platform built specifically for financial institutions, Wealth.com http://Wealth.com enables firms to drive scale, efficiency, and measurable client impact. Trusted by some of the largest names in finance, Wealth.com http://Wealth.com combines proprietary AI, robust security, and deep technological and legal expertise to serve the full range of client needs, from foundational plans to the most sophisticated estate strategies. The company has been widely recognized for innovation and leadership, winning Top Estate Planning Technology and Top Estate Planning Implementation at the 2025 WealthManagement.com http://WealthManagement.com Industry Awards, being named the 2024 Best Technology Provider in the Trust category, and earning #1 in estate planning market share in the 2025 Kitces AdvisorTech Study.

Our team is fundamental to our standing as the leading estate planning platform. We cultivate a collaborative and supportive environment, fostering innovation and making Wealth.com http://Wealth.com a truly enjoyable workplace. Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is proud to be certified as a Great Place to Work for 2025.



The Opportunity
Looking to gain practical, hands-on experience in the field of AI/ML? The http://wealth.com/Wealth.com http://Wealth.com Technology Internship Program helps passionate early career professionals gain real-world exposure to applying the latest advancements in Large Language Models (LLM), Computer Vision (CV) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) in the legal & financial tech sector.

Applied Scientist Interns work collaboratively with Software Engineers and other Applied Scientists to pursue a broad range of AI initiatives. By using a combination of various state-of-the-art natural language and vision models, interns will help build complex legal AI assistants.



Key Responsibilities

 - Work with complex datasets from various sources to build Extract, Transform, and Load (ETL) data pipelines for downstream tasks

 - Finetune and integrate the latest Large Language Models (LLMs) such as OpenAI/Gemini/MistralAI models into production systems.

 - Train, finetune and deploy large-scale NLP and CV models to power complex document understanding experiences.

 - Design and implement scalable and efficient Q&A RAG frameworks

 - Collaborate with other product managers and engineers in the team to build tools to evaluate and improve the end-to-end AI quality of our production systems.

 - Effectively communicating and demonstrating work to stakeholders



Required Qualifications

 - Currently pursuing a Bachelor's, Master’s OR PhD degree in Computer Science, Electrical or Computer Engineering, or related field (e.g., statistics, predictive analytics, research)

 - Experience building sophisticated RAG pipelines with LLMs

 - OR Experience with common machine learning, deep learning frameworks like PyTorch/TensorFlow

 - OR Experience developing and deploying complex AI/ML models to production



Preferred Qualifications

 - Currently pursuing a Master’s OR PhD degree in Computer Science, Electrical or Computer Engineering, or related field (e.g., statistics, predictive analytics, research) AND 1+ years related experience (e.g., statistics, predictive analytics, research)

 - 1+ year(s) experience productionizing complex AI/ML models

 - 1+ year(s) experience creating publications (e.g., patents, peer-reviewed academic papers).

 - 1+ year(s) experience conducting research as part of a research program (in academic or industry settings)



Compensation & Level

$45- $55/ Hour



Wealth is an equal opportunity employer and encourages people from all backgrounds to apply. Should you have a disability o

source_jobs.title: Strategic Accounts Onboarding Manager
source_jobs.description:
Title: Strategic Accounts Onboarding Manager

Location: Hybrid (NYC)

About Us

Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is the industry’s leading advanced planning platform. Trusted by many of the largest names in global finance, Wealth.com http://Wealth.com combines proprietary AI with deep technical expertise to serve the needs of clients across the wealth spectrum. The company has been widely recognized for innovation and leadership, winning Top Estate Planning Technology and Top Estate Planning Implementation at the 2025 WealthManagement.com http://WealthManagement.com Industry Awards, being named the 2024 Best Technology Provider in the Trust category, and earning #1 in estate planning market share in the 2025 Kitces AdvisorTech Study.

Our team is fundamental to our standing as the leading wealth planning platform. We cultivate a collaborative and supportive environment, fostering innovation and making Wealth.com http://Wealth.com a truly enjoyable workplace. Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is proud to be certified as a Great Place to Work for 2025.

The Opportunity
We are seeking a proven Strategic Accounts Onboarding Manager to personally lead onboarding for our most strategic accounts — and simultaneously build the program, playbooks, and infrastructure that will define how wealth.com http://wealth.com scales its onboarding function. This is a dual role: you will be both a practitioner and a builder. On one hand, you will be the primary point of contact for our highest-value enterprise clients — RIAs, broker-dealers, and private wealth firms — delivering a white-glove onboarding experience that sets the tone for long-term partnership. On the other, you will serve as a strategic thought partner to leadership, designing the frameworks, training assets, in-product resources, and playbooks that allow our Onboarding Specialists to execute consistently at scale.

This role requires deep fluency in the wealth management industry. Our strategic clients span RIA, BD, and private wealth channels — and this person needs to walk into those conversations with genuine domain expertise, not just a product script.

Key Responsibilities

 - Personally lead onboarding for strategic accounts — RIAs, broker-dealers, and private wealth firms — delivering white-glove experiences that accelerate time-to-value and drive long-term retention

 - Build and own the onboarding strategy: define the post-sale journey, white-glove service model, tiering framework, and success milestones from kickoff through full adoption

 - Develop the onboarding infrastructure — playbooks, training assets, in-product resources, client-facing documentation, and scalable frameworks — that enable Onboarding Specialists to execute consistently without reinventing the wheel on every engagement

 - Manage and develop the Onboarding team, providing coaching, clear ownership, and a structure that scales as the team and client base grows

 - Partner with Sales to ensure strategic accounts are handed off with full context — and that the transition from close to onboarding feels seamless to the client

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Customer Success, and Support to surface onboarding friction, advocate for in-product improvements, and reduce time-to-value across the client base

 - Identify opportunities to build self-service and scalable onboarding experiences — so the team can continue to deliver quality as volume grows without proportional headcount increases

 - Define and track onboarding KPIs: time-to-value, activation rates, onboarding completion rates, and early retention indicators — and use that data to continuously improve the program

Qualifications

 - 5+ years of experience in enterprise onboarding, implementation, or customer success within wealth management, fintech, or financial services SaaS

 - Deep knowledge of the RIA, broker-dealer, and/or private wealth space — you understand how these firms operate, how advisors think, and what drives adoption of n

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, AI/ML (Infrastructure & Platform)
source_jobs.description:
Role: Senior Software Engineer, AI/ML (AI Infrastructure & Platform)

Location: Hybrid, NYC




ABOUT US

Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is the industry’s leading estate planning platform, empowering more than 1,000 wealth management firms to modernize how they talk about estate planning with their clients. As the only tech-led, end-to-end platform built specifically for financial institutions, Wealth.com http://Wealth.com enables firms to drive scale, efficiency, and measurable client impact. Trusted by some of the largest names in finance, Wealth.com http://Wealth.com combines proprietary AI, robust security, and deep technological and legal expertise to serve the full range of client needs, from foundational plans to the most sophisticated estate strategies. The company has been widely recognized for innovation and leadership, winning Top Estate Planning Technology and Top Estate Planning Implementation at the 2025 WealthManagement.com http://WealthManagement.com Industry Awards, being named the 2024 Best Technology Provider in the Trust category, and earning #1 in estate planning market share in the 2025 Kitces AdvisorTech Study.

Our team is fundamental to our standing as the leading estate planning platform. We cultivate a collaborative and supportive environment, fostering innovation and making Wealth.com http://Wealth.com a truly enjoyable workplace. Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is proud to be certified as a Great Place to Work for 2025.

 
 


THE ROLE

We are seeking a Software Engineer, AI/ML (Infrastructure & Platform) to build the foundational systems that power our next generation of AI applications.

This is a systems-focused role. You will design and build the platforms, abstractions, and infrastructure that enable teams to reliably develop, deploy, and scale AI systems — including agentic workflows, retrieval pipelines, and model integrations.

You will operate at the intersection of AI systems and distributed infrastructure, focusing on the “how” behind production AI: how models are orchestrated, how tools/skills are exposed and executed, and how systems are evaluated, monitored, and scaled in real-world environments.

Your work will directly enable product teams to move faster while ensuring our AI systems are reliable, observable, secure, and cost-efficient.

 
 


WHAT YOU WILL DO


BUILD CORE AI INFRASTRUCTURE

 - Design and implement platforms for LLM orchestration, tool execution, and agent workflows

 - Develop shared services and abstractions used across multiple AI applications


BUILD AI CAPABILITY LAYERS (TOOLS / SKILLS)

 - Design and implement tools (“skills”) that agents and applications rely on, including APIs, workflows, and integrations

 - Define clear interfaces for capabilities such as data retrieval, calculations, document processing, and external system actions

 - Build reusable, composable abstractions that enable safe and scalable tool usage across systems

 - Ensure tools are reliable, observable, and secure, especially when interacting with sensitive data


ENABLE AGENTIC SYSTEMS AT SCALE

 - Build infrastructure to support multi-step agents (state management, tool routing, retries, failure handling)

 - Design systems where agents reason over and invoke tools/skills reliably

 - Create reusable orchestration patterns between models and capabilities


DEVELOP EVALUATION AND OBSERVABILITY SYSTEMS

 - Build frameworks for offline and online evaluation of AI systems

 - Implement logging, tracing, and monitoring for model behavior and system performance


OWN RELIABILITY AND PERFORMANCE

 - Design systems for high availability, fault tolerance, and graceful degradation

 - Optimize for latency, throughput, and cost across AI workloads


BUILD DATA AND RETRIEVAL INFRASTRUCTURE

 - Develop scalable RAG pipelines, indexing systems, and data processing workflows

 - Own infrastructure for handling large-scale structured and unstructured data


CREATE INTERNAL PLATFORMS AND DEVELOPER TOOLING

source_jobs.title: Account Manager, Institutional
source_jobs.description:
Title: Account Manager, Institutional
Location: Hybrid, NYC



About Us

Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is the industry’s leading estate planning platform, empowering more than 1,000 wealth management firms to modernize the delivery of estate planning guidance to their clients. As the only tech-led, end-to-end platform built specifically for financial institutions, Wealth.com http://Wealth.com enables firms to drive scale, efficiency, and measurable client impact. Trusted by some of the largest names in finance, Wealth.com http://Wealth.com combines proprietary AI, robust security, and deep technological and legal expertise to serve the full range of client needs, from foundational plans to the most sophisticated estate strategies. The company has been widely recognized for innovation and leadership, winning Top Estate Planning Technology and Top Estate Planning Implementation at the 2025 WealthManagement.com http://WealthManagement.com Industry Awards, being named the 2024 Best Technology Provider in the Trust category, and earning #1 in estate planning market share in the 2025 Kitces AdvisorTech Study.

Our team is fundamental to our standing as the leading estate planning platform. We cultivate a collaborative and supportive environment, fostering innovation and making Wealth.com http://Wealth.com a truly enjoyable workplace. Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is proud to be certified as a Great Place to Work for 2025.


The Opportunity

We are seeking an Account Manager to support some of our largest institutional clients, including leading national banking organizations. This individual will play a key role in managing day-to-day client relationships, coordinating across internal teams, and ensuring seamless deployment and adoption of our platform.

The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced environment, excels at cross-functional collaboration, and consistently delivers a high-touch client experience. This is a unique opportunity to sit at the center of Wealth.com http://Wealth.com’s client operations, partnering with top-tier institutions and contributing directly to the success and growth of our enterprise relationships.



Key Responsibilities

 - Serve as the primary day-to-day contact for assigned institutional accounts.

 - Manage onboarding and deployment of new users, ensuring a smooth rollout and adoption across client teams.

 - Coordinate with Product, Engineering, Support, and Compliance teams to resolve client issues and implement workflow improvements.

 - Track and maintain organized workflows, timelines, and client documentation.

 - Monitor platform usage, identify opportunities to drive engagement, and escalate risks proactively.

 - Support client meetings, QBRs, and other reviews with preparation of materials and actionable insights.

 - Document processes and share knowledge internally to improve team efficiency and scalability.

 - Act as a trusted advisor, anticipating client needs and providing proactive solutions to enhance satisfaction and retention.



Qualifications

 - Experience: 5+ years in client management, account management, or operations within financial services, wealth management, or technology-driven environments.

 - Preferred Experience: Prior experience managing client relationships with institutional banks or large financial institutions; experience with estate planning is strongly preferred.

 - Proven track record of managing high-value or complex client accounts and driving measurable results.

 - Demonstrated success in cross-functional collaboration, problem-solving, and process improvement.

 - Deep understanding of financial services workflows, client engagement strategies, or enterprise technology platforms.

 - Skills: Strong organizational skills, excellent verbal and written communication, technical fluency with SaaS platforms, and ability to manage multiple concurrent workstreams.

 - Other: High emotional intelligence, attention to detail, client-first mindset, and a proac

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is the industry's leading estate planning platform, empowering more than 1,000 wealth management firms to modernize the delivery of estate planning guidance to their clients. As the only tech-led, end-to-end platform built specifically for financial institutions, Wealth.com http://Wealth.com enables firms to drive scale, efficiency, and measurable client impact. Trusted by some of the largest names in finance, Wealth.com http://Wealth.com combines proprietary AI, robust security, and deep technological and legal expertise to serve the full range of client needs, from foundational plans to the most sophisticated estate strategies. The company has been widely recognized for innovation and leadership, winning Top Estate Planning Technology and Top Estate Planning Implementation at the 2025 WealthManagement.com http://WealthManagement.com Industry Awards, being named the 2024 Best Technology Provider in the Trust category, and earning #1 in estate planning market share in the 2025 Kitces AdvisorTech Study.

Our team is fundamental to our standing as the leading estate planning platform. We cultivate a collaborative and supportive environment, fostering innovation and making Wealth.com http://Wealth.com a truly enjoyable workplace. Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is proud to be certified as a Great Place to Work for 2025.




THE OPPORTUNITY

As a Software Engineer at Wealth.com http://Wealth.com, you will help design, build, and scale the backend systems powering our estate planning, tax planning, and AI-driven products used by tens of thousands of financial advisors and their clients. You'll work on a TypeScript-first stack alongside engineers who have shipped production software at scale, and you'll have significant ownership over the systems you build — from architecture and implementation through deployment, monitoring, and iteration.

We're hiring across multiple product areas (estate planning, tax planning, Esther AI, platform/integrations) and will match strong engineers to the team where they're most excited to contribute.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design, build, and operate backend services that power Wealth.com http://Wealth.com's products — including APIs, data pipelines, and integrations with enterprise wealth management platforms

 - Take features from spec through design, implementation, testing, deployment, and post-launch monitoring; own what you ship

 - Write production-grade TypeScript code with strong testing, observability, and operational discipline

 - Collaborate with product managers, designers, and other engineers to translate ambiguous problems into well-scoped technical solutions

 - Contribute to system design decisions around scalability, reliability, security, and performance

 - Participate in code review, technical design discussions, and on-call rotations as the team grows

 - Help shape engineering culture, tooling, and best practices as we scale the org




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3+ years of professional software engineering experience shipping backend systems to production

 - Strong proficiency in TypeScript, or deep experience in another typed language (Java, Go, C#, Kotlin, Rust) with demonstrated ability to ramp quickly into TypeScript

 - Hands-on experience designing and operating systems at meaningful scale — handling real traffic, real data volumes, and real users

 - Solid grasp of backend fundamentals: API design (REST/gRPC), relational and NoSQL databases, messaging/event-driven systems, caching, and concurrency

 - Experience with cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, or Azure), containerization (Docker/Kubernetes), and modern CI/CD workflows

 - Production-grade operational mindset: logging, metrics, tracing, alerting, incident response, and a track record of supporting what you ship

 - Comfortable working in a fast-paced, ambiguous, startup environment where priorities shift and you're expected to drive your own work

 - Strong written and verbal com

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative - West Coast
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Wealth.com http://wealth.com/ is the industry’s leading AI Wealth Management platform, empowering financial advisors to offer modern, comprehensive estate planning solutions to their clients. By combining cutting-edge technology, AI, and unmatched human expertise, we deliver a seamless experience that sets us apart. Beyond having one of the most coveted domain names in FinTech, wealth.com http://wealth.com is a dynamic, revenue-generating startup experiencing rapid yet sustainable growth.

 

A key reason behind our position as the #1 estate planning platform is our team. Our people thrive in a collaborative, supportive culture that fosters innovation and makes wealth.com http://wealth.com a place where everyone truly enjoys working. Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is certified as a Great Place to Work for 2024 and named a 2024 finalist for Fast Company’s Best Workplaces for Innovators!

 

The ideal Support Associate both enjoys, and has experience in, the customer service space. This may be through face-to-face channels, such as retail, or over the phone, live chats, or emails. You enjoy working with people, both as customers, and within a tight knit team who create experiences our customers tell others about. You are motivated by learnings and new challenges and are looking for a role that can set you on a range of career paths.

 


POSITION OVERVIEW:

We are seeking a motivated and inclusive Business Development Representative to join our team. In this role, you will focus on managing high-volume of both inbound leads and outbound business development, ensuring a client-first approach. Your efforts will directly contribute to our growth and success in the wealth management sector.

 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Collaborate with Sales, Marketing, and Product teams to execute strategies for managing and converting inbound leads.

 - Regularly perform against individual monthly and/or quarterly targets: pipeline creation, conversion rates, appointments set, and follow-up calls assigned to quota-carrying sales team.

 - Collaborate with Sales and other stakeholders on reporting and analysis.

 - Ensure CRM tools and value-selling methodologies are leveraged to process and track opportunities.

 - Generate qualified meetings and sales pipeline within strategic and targeted accounts.

 - Manage target account lists and drive a comprehensive demand generation plan.

 - Maintain a high level of daily activity, including calls, email, and LinkedIn outreach.

 - Use advanced prospecting tools such as LinkedIn, Zoominfo, and other tools to uncover new opportunities and build trust with prospects.

 - Keep up to date with the financial technology industry and competitive landscape.

 - Demonstrate a clear understanding of sales processes and product and service value propositions.

 - Prioritize client needs and ensure a positive experience throughout the sales process.

 


QUALIFICATIONS:

 - Bachelor’s degree or equivalent relevant work experience required.

 - 3 years of experience working as a SaaS business or sales development representative looking to progress their career.

 - Solid understanding of Salesforce, Outreach.io, and Zoominfo.

 - Strong history of achieving and exceeding targets.

 - Experience working both inbound and outbound channels.

 - Proficiency in cold calling, email personalization, and social selling.

 - Passion for team collaboration and helping others succeed.A client-first mentality with a focus on delivering exceptional service.
   
    


BENEFITS & PERKS

 - Hybrid in New York City Office

 - Excellent Medical, Dental, and Vision insurance options, with low-cost premium structures that demonstrate our commitment to offering great value to our employees.

 - 100% Company Paid Basic Life Insurance, Short Term and Long Term Disability

 - 100% Paid Parental Leave upon eligibility

 - Company equity managed through Carta.

 - 401k with Match and 100% vesting upon hire.

 - Flexible PTO in an

source_jobs.title: GTM Engineer
source_jobs.description:

Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is certified as a Great Place to Work for 2024 and named a 2024 finalist for Fast Company’s Best Workplaces for Innovators!



About the Role

Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is looking for a GTM Engineer to build and maintain the automation and integration layer that powers our revenue operations. You will report directly to the Director of Revenue Operations, GTM Systems, and be the hands-on builder who turns strategy into working systems.

This is a technical execution role. You will spend your time building, configuring, debugging, and optimizing across a modern GTM stack. If you like seeing the thing you built actually ship and make a team faster, this role is for you.



What You'll Own

Automation & Integrations

 - Build and maintain automated workflows across our GTM stack.

 - Own the integration layer between Salesforce, Outreach, Gong, ChiliPiper, DealHub, and other tools in the stack

 - Build and maintain API connections, webhooks, and data syncs that keep our systems in agreement

 - Identify and automate manual processes across the sales and marketing motion

Salesforce Administration

 - Handle day-to-day Salesforce administration including object configuration, flows, workflow rules, and permission sets

 - Support data hygiene initiatives including deduplication, enrichment, and validation rules

 - Assist in implementing data model changes as the business evolves

AI-Assisted Workflows

 - Implement LLM-assisted automations including call summaries, enrichment pipelines, and follow-up draft generation

 - Maintain and iterate on prompt-based workflows under the direction of the Director

 - Evaluate and integrate AI tools into existing workflows as the stack grows

Revenue Data & Reporting

 - Own the accuracy of data flowing between systems including audit processes and alerting for data breaks

 - Build and maintain enrichment pipelines that keep account and contact data current

 - Support attribution reporting and pipeline analytics



What We're Looking For

Required

 - 2-4 years in a technical RevOps or sales systems role with direct ownership of Salesforce administration, workflow automation, or GTM integrations.

 - Salesforce proficiency including flows, reports, custom objects, and core admin tasks

 - Experience building AI-assisted workflows or working with LLM integrations

 - Comfortable working with APIs, webhooks, and basic data transformation.

 - Detail-oriented with strong data instincts. You catch errors before they propagate.

 - Background in B2B SaaS.

Preferred

 - Experience with Outreach, Gong, ChiliPiper, and/or DealHub

 - Basic scripting ability in Python or JavaScript

 - Familiarity with SQL or BI tools (Omni, Looker, or similar)

 - Experience in fintech, or wealth management



What Success Looks Like

In your first 60 days, you will have mapped the existing integration layer, documented what exists, and shipped at least one automation that saves the team measurable time. Within six months, you will be the person the sales team calls when something breaks and the person the Director trusts to execute the roadmap without hand-holding.

You are a builder. You move fast, test before you ship, document what you build, and c

About the Role

Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is building revenue infrastructure to scale. As our Director of Revenue Operations, GTM Systems, you will own the systems, data, and automation layer that powers how we acquire, convert, and retain clients. This is a builder and operator role. You will design the architecture, run the tooling, push AI-native workflows into production, and stay in the weeds on the day-to-day processes that keep the revenue engine running.

You will work directly with sales, marketing, finance, and leadership. You will move fast, ship real things, and make the revenue engine materially better.



What You'll Own

GTM Systems Architecture

 - Design and own the full GTM tech stack: Salesforce, Gong, DealHub, Outreach, ChiliPiper/Qualified, and the automation layer connecting them

Preferred

 - Experience with DealHub, Outreach, ChiliPiper, and/or Qualified

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, DevOps
source_jobs.description:
Title: Senior Software Engineer, DevOps

Location: Hybrid — New York, NY



About Us

Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is the industry’s leading AI wealth management platform, empowering financial institutions to modernize how they deliver planning advice to clients. Trusted by the largest firms in wealth management, Wealth.com http://Wealth.com combines proprietary AI, enterprise-grade security, and deep legal and tax expertise to power scalable, compliant, and personalized planning experiences across the full client lifecycle.



The company has been widely recognized for innovation and leadership, winning Top Estate Planning Technology and Top Estate Planning Implementation at the 2025 WealthManagement.com http://WealthManagement.com Industry Awards, and earning #1 in estate planning market share in the 2025 Kitces AdvisorTech Study.



At Wealth.com http://Wealth.com, our team is core to everything we build. We foster a collaborative, high-performing environment that values innovation, ownership, and continuous improvement. Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is proud to be certified as a Great Place to Work®.



Overview

As a Senior DevOps Engineer, you will design and manage our cloud infrastructure, primarily on AWS with some GCP integration, focusing on cloud-native architectures and multi-region configurations. You will leverage AWS CDK, Terraform, and CI/CD pipelines to ensure scalability, resiliency, performance, and cost-effectiveness for our serverless platform, while implementing robust monitoring and log management solutions.



Key Responsibilities

 - Architect and maintain cloud-native infrastructure on AWS (Lambda, AppSync, DynamoDB, RDS, S3) and GCP (GKE, Cloud Run) using AWS CDK and Terraform, emphasizing multi-region setups for scalability, resiliency, and performance.

 - Design and implement CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions and AWS CodePipeline for efficient, reliable deployments. Build serverless and containerized solutions to support scalable, cost-effective systems.

 - Optimize multi-region configurations to balance high availability, fault tolerance, and cost efficiency while meeting compliance (e.g., SOC 2, PCI DSS).

 - Implement and manage monitoring and log management solutions using tools like http://logz.ioDatadog, Cloudwatch Logs, ensure observability and rapid issue resolution.

 - Collaborate with developers to integrate DevOps practices, including automated testing, monitoring, and observability.

 - Collaborate with engineering and security teams to ensure robust infrastructure security and compliance.

 - Troubleshoot and resolve infrastructure and application issues across production and non-production environments.

 - Drive cloud cost optimization strategies and adopt best practices for performance, resiliency, and log management.



Required Skills and Qualifications

 - 5+ years in DevOps or SRE roles, ideally in SaaS or fintech.

 - Expertise in AWS (Lambda, DynamoDB, RDS, S3) and working knowledge of GCP (GKE).

 - Proficiency with AWS CDK (TypeScript/Python) and Terraform for Infrastructure-as-Code.

 - Strong experience with CI/CD tools (GitHub Actions, AWS CodePipeline).

 - Deep understanding of cloud-native architectures and multi-region configurations for scalability, resiliency, performance, and cost optimization.

 - Hands-on experience with serverless architectures and container orchestration (GKE).

 - Proficiency in monitoring and log management tools, including Datadog, Cloudwatch Logs.

 - Knowledge of cloud security, networking, and compliance standards.

 - Scripting skills (Python, Bash, Powershell) for automation.

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or equivalent experience.



Preferred Qualifications

 - SaaS experience with regulatory knowledge (SOC2, PCI DSS).

 - Expertise in SRE principles and experience managing highly available systems.

 - AWS or GCP certifications (e.g., AWS DevOps Engineer, Solutions Architect)

 - Active GitHub profile or open-source c

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Designer, AI-Native Product
source_jobs.description:
Senior Product Designer, AI-Native Product
Location: New York, NY (Hybrid, 3 days a week in office)



About Wealth.com http://Wealth.com

Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is the industry’s leading AI Wealth Management platform, empowering financial advisors to offer modern, comprehensive estate planning solutions to their clients. By combining cutting-edge technology, AI, and unmatched human expertise, we deliver a seamless experience that sets us apart. Beyond having one of the most coveted domain names in FinTech, wealth.com http://wealth.com is a dynamic, revenue-generating startup experiencing rapid yet sustainable growth.

A key reason behind our position as the #1 estate planning platform is our team. Our people thrive in a collaborative, supportive culture that fosters innovation and makes wealth.com http://wealth.com a place where everyone truly enjoys working. Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is certified as a Great Place to Work for 2024 and named a 2024 finalist for Fast Company’s Best Workplaces for Innovators!

People don’t remember the feature. They remember how it made them feel. An advisor guiding a family through estate and tax planning does not want a more complicated tool. They want to feel calm, capable, and sure. That is what we design for.

We believe the best technology earns trust by getting out of the way. When intelligence is designed with care, people stop thinking about the tool and focus on what they came to do. Our job is to make the hard things feel simple.




HOW WE WORK

We design from the person out. We study how someone thinks, what they are trying to do, and where the experience gets in their way, then craft every detail around that.

We are pioneering what AI-native design actually means. Most teams are still adding AI on top of old patterns. We are defining new ones. Intelligence should carry the weight, so the person is free to think about the decision, not the mechanics of getting there.

We author the frame, name the owners, and step back. We trust designers to hold the quality bar together, from day one, not as a reward we hand out slowly. You will have real ownership of problems that matter, shaping a product where design quality decides whether people trust us with the most important decisions of their lives.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

You will own real problems end to end. You will take an ambiguous need, frame it, and ship the experience that makes value land in the first few moments. Activation matters here, because the sooner someone feels capable, the sooner they trust us.

You will design for intelligence. You will shape how AI manages and surfaces complex data, and how we build on top of it so the product feels effortless. Much of the work means taking dense financial and legal information and designing an experience that lets a person grasp it in seconds. The goal is a product so easy to use that advisors stop thinking about the software and focus entirely on their clients.

This is a senior individual contributor role. You lead through your work and your influence, not by managing people. You will raise the standard around you, give feedback that sharpens other designers' work, and help the team hold its own bar.




WHO YOU ARE

You have designed AI-native products, where the intelligence is the heart of the experience, not a feature added on top. You are as comfortable taking something complex and making it feel simple as you are starting from a blank page and shaping a 0-to-1 experience out of ambiguity.

Beyond that, you are someone who:

 - Thinks in systems and cares about craft, moving easily between the big frame and the smallest detail.

 - Treats AI as a design material, not a feature. You are curious about how models behave and you prototype with real intelligence, not just static screens.

 - Owns problems from framing through shipped detail, without needing them fully defined for you.

 - Thinks like a product owner. You understand the strategy, the metrics, and the roadma

Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is the industry's leading AI Wealth Management platform, empowering financial advisors to offer modern, comprehensive estate planning solutions to their clients. By combining cutting-edge technology, AI, and unmatched human expertise, we deliver a seamless experience that sets us apart. People don't remember the feature. They remember how it made them feel. An advisor guiding a family through estate and tax planning does not want a more complicated tool. They want to feel calm, capable, and sure. That is what we design for.

We believe the best technology earns trust by getting out of the way. When intelligence is designed with care, people stop thinking about the tool and focus on what they came to do. Our job is to make the hard things feel simple.



Role Overview

As the Senior Manager of Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) at Wealth.com http://Wealth.com, you will report directly to and work closely with the Chief Financial Officer. You will be tasked with transforming raw financial and operational data into narrative-driven strategy, dissecting the issues affecting the company's key growth initiatives, and assisting with other strategic objectives. You will actively partner with leadership, moving past static spreadsheet data to deliver real-time, data-driven insights that steer corporate strategy. This is a hybrid role where you will be expected to work from the NYC office a minimum of three days per week.



What You'll Do

 - Help design, implement, and manage the company's continuous rolling forecast and financial planning processes

 - Champion the use of modern AI workflows (specifically leveraging Claude and Gemini) to automate manual inputs, streamline coding scripts for data manipulation, and run real-time scenario planning

 - Construct sophisticated, scalable financial models from scratch to backstop proposed business development, pricing strategies, and core corporate strategic initiatives

 - Gather, synthesize, and translate complex financial results into clear, narrative-driven insights and presentations for the leadership team and Board of Directors

 - Collaborate closely with functional departments to aggregate financial and operational data, map business trends, and identify critical optimization opportunities

 - Partner directly with the Accounting team to streamline monthly, quarterly, and year-end close cycles by ensuring tight integration between financial models and actual system results

 - Manage key inputs for the company's operating model

 - Support the Sales and Product teams through deep financial analysis

 - Prepare reports for the leadership team

 - Assist the Accounting team with monthly, quarterly, and year-end closings of the books and records



What You'll Bring

 - Bachelor's degree with a major in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a quantitative field (e.g., Data Science)

 - Minimum 4-6 years of progressive experience, ideally with an investment banking or management consulting background and more recent FP&A experience within a technology start-up

 - Ability to thrive in an individual contributor capacity where you will own the entire workflow from data extraction to executive synthesis

 - High tolerance for ambiguity and ability to manage changing priorities dynamically

 - Strong comfort with and curiosity for integrating generative AI tools (specifical

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is the industry's leading advanced planning platform. Trusted by many of the largest names in global finance, Wealth.com http://Wealth.com combines proprietary AI with deep technical expertise to serve the needs of clients across the wealth spectrum. The company has been widely recognized for innovation and leadership, winning Top Estate Planning Technology and Top Estate Planning Implementation at the 2025 WealthManagement.com http://WealthManagement.com Industry Awards, being named the 2024 Best Technology Provider in the Trust category, and earning #1 in estate planning market share in the 2025 Kitces AdvisorTech Study.



Our team is fundamental to our standing as the leading wealth planning platform. We cultivate a collaborative and supportive environment, fostering innovation and making Wealth.com http://Wealth.com a truly enjoyable workplace. Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is proud to be certified as a Great Place to Work for 2025.



The Opportunity

We are seeking a Senior Product Marketing Manager to join our growing Product Marketing team, reporting directly to the Director of Product Marketing. Product Marketing at Wealth.com http://Wealth.com sits within Product (not Marketing), and owns go-to-market strategy, positioning and messaging, sales enablement, lifecycle marketing, and product launches across our platform.



In this role, you'll focus on our enterprise segment: the large institutions, RIAs, and wealth management firms that represent our most strategic clients. You'll own positioning and go-to-market strategy for this segment, lead launch execution for new features and products, and act as the connective tissue between Product, Sales, and Customer Success to make sure our enterprise clients understand and adopt what we build.



This is a hands-on role. You'll be expected to execute (not just strategize) across email campaigns, launch assets, sales enablement content, and competitive positioning, while increasingly leveraging AI tools to move faster and do more with a lean team.



Key Responsibilities

 - Own positioning, messaging, and go-to-market strategy for Wealth.com http://Wealth.com's product suite, with a growing focus on our enterprise segment as you ramp into the role

 - Lead end-to-end product launches from positioning and messaging to internal enablement and external communications

 - Partner cross-functionally with Product, Sales, and Customer Success to ensure new features and products land clearly with enterprise clients and drive adoption

 - Build and execute lifecycle and email marketing campaigns that support activation, adoption, and retention across the client journey

 - Develop and maintain sales enablement assets (competitive battlecards, talk tracks, and collateral) that help our sales and CS teams win and grow enterprise accounts

 - Conduct competitive analysis and voice-of-customer research to sharpen positioning and inform roadmap and GTM decisions

 - Leverage AI tools to accelerate research, content development, and campaign execution, continuously looking for ways to do more with a lean team

 - Represent the voice of the enterprise customer internally, ensuring their needs and feedback shape both product and go-to-market strategy



Qualifications

 - 3–6 years of experience in product marketing, ideally within B2B SaaS

 - Experience developing differentiated positioning and messaging frameworks, and translating complex product functionality into clear, compelling narratives for different audiences

 - Hands-on email marketing execution experience, building, testing, and optimizing campaigns in platforms like Customer.io http://Customer.io, HubSpot, or similar

 - Data-driven approach to the role: comfortable defining and tracking launch effectiveness, feature adoption, and other GTM metrics, and using that data to inform decisions

 - Demonstrated comfort using AI tools to work faster and more effectively, for research, content c

Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is the industry's leading AI Wealth Management platform, empowering financial advisors to offer modern, comprehensive estate planning solutions to their clients. By combining cutting-edge technology, AI, and unmatched human expertise, we deliver a seamless experience that sets us apart. Beyond having one of the most coveted domain names in FinTech, wealth.com http://wealth.com is a dynamic, revenue-generating startup experiencing rapid yet sustainable growth.

A key reason behind our position as the #1 estate planning platform is our team. Our people thrive in a collaborative, supportive culture that fosters innovation and makes wealth.com http://wealth.com a place where everyone truly enjoys working. Wealth.com http://Wealth.com is certified as a Great Place to Work for 2024 and named a 2024 finalist for Fast Company's Best Workplaces for Innovators!



Role Overview

As a Senior Data Analyst, Product, you will partner with our product team to strengthen data-informed decision making by defining sharp success metrics. You will dive deep into our transformation and reporting platforms to improve self-service through AI and traditional dashboards, while also delivering ad hoc analyses to guide product development.



What You'll Do

 - Develop and maintain key performance metrics and dashboards to track success metrics across our product

 - Analyze product usage and revenue data to identify trends, opportunities, and areas for improvement

 - Accelerate AI self-service in our intelligence layer: Omni and Amplitude

 - Research and implement best practices to enhance AI reporting accuracy and reliability

 - Provide actionable insights to senior leadership and business stakeholders to inform decision-making

 - Refine ambiguous questions and generate new insights through a deep understanding of the data, our customers, and our business

 - Ability to translate technical and quantitative analyses into digestible and actionable recommendations



What You'll Bring

 - 4+ years of experience in analytics or strategic role at a technology company

 - Strong SQL and dbt skills

 - Prior experience with Omni (or similar BI tools)

 - Understanding of SaaS business models and product metrics

 - Very strong organizational skills

 - Strong communication skills

 - Experience optimizing context for and output of AI analytical systems



Benefits & Perks

 - Hybrid work arrangement in New York City (3x a week)

 - Excellent medical, dental, and vision insurance options, with low-cost premium structures that demonstrate our commitment to offering great value to our employees.

 - 100% company-paid basic life insurance, short-term and long-term disability insurance.

 - 100% paid parental leave upon eligibility.

 - Company equity managed through Carta.

 - 401k with match and 100% vesting upon hire.

 - Flexible PTO in an environment where taking time off to relax or recharge is supported and encouraged.

 - Take time off for holidays—and yes, your birthday counts too. Celebrate, relax, and recharge without thinking twice.

 - Free estate planning for life!

Wealth is an equal opportunity employer and encourages people from all backgrounds to apply. Should you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know.

notes: Strong tech presence, AI/ML focus, and rapid growth suggest an engineering-heavy startup
